factors.summary: Demand trends are stable with slight acceleration in certain regions.

evidence.text: Sales in our North America segment increased approximately eight percent in the third quarter of 2016 driven primarily by higher volumes of U.S. residential and commercial water heaters.

factors.summary: Order backlog is stable as new orders are consistent with revenue.

evidence.text: We expect slower growth in our Lochinvar-branded sales than we anticipated earlier in 2016. Our Lochinvar-branded boiler products have historically grown as a result of the transition from lower efficiency boilers to higher efficiency boilers, new product introductions and market share gains.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices in certain regions, particularly in North America and Rest of World, which is contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: The increase in sales in the first nine months of 2016 was primarily due to higher prices in the U.S. and Canada that were partially offset by lower residential volumes of water heaters in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, particularly in North America and Rest of World segments.

evidence.text: The increase in sales in the third quarter of 2016 was primarily due to higher sales of water heaters and water treatment products in China as well as higher residential and commercial volumes of water heaters in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating slightly, particularly in the Lochinvar-branded products, but overall growth is still positive.

evidence.text: We expect slower growth in our Lochinvar-branded sales than we anticipated earlier in 2016. This likely will result in minimal Lochinvar-branded product sales growth in 2016, which is below our most recent growth expectations of six percent.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in North America and China, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Sales in our North America segment increased approximately eight percent in the third quarter of 2016 driven primarily by higher volumes of U.S. residential and commercial water heaters. Sales in our Rest of World segment grew approximately 11 percent in the third quarter of 2016, as a result of 11 percent sales growth in China.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are in China, driven by water heater and water treatment product demand, while growth in the U.S. is more moderate.

evidence.text: China sales in local currency grew approximately 17 percent in the third quarter compared to the same period last year. We expect full year 2016 sales in China to grow at a rate in excess of 16 percent in local currency driven by expected continued overall water heater market growth, market share gains, improved product mix and growth in sales of water treatment products at a rate significantly higher than 16 percent.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable or declining, particularly in steel costs, which is benefiting margins.

evidence.text: Gross margin in the first nine months of 2016 benefitted from higher volumes in China and higher prices on water heaters in the U.S. and Canada as well as lower steel costs globally.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant pressure on margins.

evidence.text: Our pension costs and credits are developed from actuarial valuations. The valuations reflect key assumptions regarding, among other things, discount rates, expected return on assets, retirement ages, and years of service. We consider current market conditions including changes in interest rates in making these assumptions.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to higher volumes, price increases, and lower input costs.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin in the third quarter of 2016 of 41.4 percent was higher than the gross profit margin of 40.9 percent in the third quarter of 2015. Gross margin in the first nine months of 2016 increased to 41.7 percent from 39.4 percent in the first nine months of 2015.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Cost actions are delivering measurable savings, particularly in the ERP implementation and pension contributions.

evidence.text: We estimate that our fourth quarter 2016 ERP expenses will be approximately $7 million higher than the same period last year. We recognized pension income in the first nine months of 2016 of $4.8 million compared to $0.1 million of pension expense in the first nine months of 2015.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by higher volumes and efficiency gains.

evidence.text: Gross margin in the first nine months of 2016 benefitted from higher volumes in China and higher prices on water heaters in the U.S. and Canada as well as lower steel costs globally.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable, with no significant disruptions reported.

evidence.text: We expect to complete the conversion of nearly all of our North American plant sites to our new enterprise resource planning (ERP) system by the end of 2016.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, particularly in support of growth in China and the U.S.

evidence.text: We project 2016 capital expenditures will be approximately $95 million to $100 million, including approximately $10 million to support our ERP system implementation and approximately $27 million related to capacity expansion to support growth in China and the U.S.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, with higher balances partially offset by higher accounts payable.

evidence.text: Working capital of $825.5 million at September 30, 2016 was $63.3 million higher than at December 31, 2015 primarily due to higher cash and marketable securities balances located outside of the U.S., higher global inventory balances and higher working capital resulting from the acquisition of Aquasana which were partially offset by higher accounts payable balances in China.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are stable, with no significant incidents reported.

evidence.text: Our pension plan continues to meet all funding requirements under Employee Retirement Income Security Act regulations.

factors.summary: Forward guidance has been maintained or slightly increased, reflecting confidence in future performance.

evidence.text: We expect our sales to grow between eight and 8.25 percent in local currency and between six and 6.25 percent in U.S. dollars in 2016. These estimates do not include the potential impact from future acquisitions.

factors.summary: Management confidence is high, as indicated by increased guidance and continued investments.

evidence.text: As a result of continued strong performance in China and in North America, we increased the midpoint of our guidance for 2016. We believe the combination of available borrowing capacity and operating cash flow will provide sufficient funds to finance our existing operations for the foreseeable future.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential currency fluctuations and geopolitical factors, but these are manageable.

evidence.text: We expect China sales growth at a rate greater than 16 percent in local currency in 2016. Foreign currency fluctuations; our inability to implement and maintain pricing actions; potential weakening in the U.S. residential or commercial construction or instability in our replacement markets; uncertain costs, savings and timeframes associated with our implementation of our new enterprise resource planning system; foreign currency fluctuations; our ability to execute our acquisition strategy; competitive pressures on our businesses; and adverse general economic conditions and capital market deterioration.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Forward Looking Statements

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, with investments in ERP systems and capacity expansion to support growth.

evidence.text: We project 2016 capital expenditures will be approximately $95 million to $100 million, including approximately $10 million to support our ERP system implementation and approximately $27 million related to capacity expansion to support growth in China and the U.S.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange movements, commodity prices, and interest rates.

evidence.text: These estimates do not include the potential impact from future acquisitions. Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from these expectations include, among other things, the following: a further slowdown in the growth rate of the Chinese economy and/or a decline in the growth rate of consumer spending in China; potential weakening in the high efficiency boiler segment in the U.S.; significant volatility in raw material prices; our inability to implement and maintain pricing actions; potential weakening in the U.S. residential or commercial construction or instability in our replacement markets; uncertain costs, savings and timeframes associated with our implementation of our new enterprise resource planning system; foreign currency fluctuations; our ability to execute our acquisition strategy; competitive pressures on our businesses; and adverse general economic conditions and capital market deterioration.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Forward Looking Statements

factors.summary: Specific catalysts include new product launches and successful ERP implementation.

evidence.text: We expect to complete the conversion of nearly all of our North American plant sites to our new enterprise resource planning (ERP) system by the end of 2016. We estimate that our annual effective income tax rate for the full year 2016 will be approximately 30 percent, compared to 29.7 percent for the full year 2015.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, with growth in key markets supporting the business.

evidence.text: We expect full year 2016 sales in China to grow at a rate in excess of 16 percent in local currency driven by expected continued overall water heater market growth, market share gains, improved product mix and growth in sales of water treatment products at a rate significantly higher than 16 percent.

factors.summary: Interest rate impact is manageable, with a slight increase in interest expenses but overall stable.

evidence.text: Our effective income tax rates for the third quarter and first nine months of 2016 were 29.7 percent and 29.6 percent, respectively. Our effective income tax rates for the third quarter and first nine months of 2015 were 31.3 percent and 30.7 percent, respectively.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: FX exposure is a headwind due to currency translation effects, particularly in China.

evidence.text: The increase in sales in the third quarter of 2016 was primarily due to higher sales of water heaters and water treatment products in China as well as higher residential and commercial volumes of water heaters in the U.S. The increase in sales the first nine months of 2016 was primarily due to higher sales of water heaters and water treatment products in China as well as higher prices in the U.S. and Canada that were partially offset by lower residential volumes of water heaters in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2016 COMPARED TO 2015

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are present but not material, with no significant disruptions reported.

evidence.text: Our pension plan continues to meet all funding requirements under Employee Retirement Income Security Act regulations.

factors.summary: CapEx outlook is stable to increasing, particularly in support of growth and ERP implementation.

evidence.text: We project 2016 capital expenditures will be approximately $95 million to $100 million, including approximately $10 million to support our ERP system implementation and approximately $27 million related to capacity expansion to support growth in China and the U.S.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing slightly due to the Aquasana acquisition, but remains manageable.

evidence.text: Our total debt increased $87.2 million from $249.0 million at December 31, 2015 to $336.2 million at September 30, 2016, primarily due to the acquisition of Aquasana. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, was 18.1 percent at September 30, 2016, compared with 14.7 percent at December 31, 2015.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing with a stock buyback program and dividend payments, signaling management confidence.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we anticipate spending approximately $135 million on stock repurchases in 2016. On October 6, 2016, our Board of Directors declared a regular cash dividend of $0.12 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with higher operating cash flows and manageable capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities in the first nine months of 2016 increased to $263.6 million compared with $237.1 million of cash provided by operating activities during the same period last year. For the full year 2016, we expect total cash provided by operating activities to be approximately $325 million.

factors.summary: MA activity is positive with the acquisition of Aquasana contributing to growth.

evidence.text: On August 8, 2016, we entered the U.S. water treatment market by acquiring Aquasana. Aquasana is included in our North America segment and added $6.2 million of sales in that segment.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes are not a significant factor, with the company meeting all necessary compliance requirements.

evidence.text: Our pension plan continues to meet all funding requirements under Employee Retirement Income Security Act regulations.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is in order, with no significant costs or operational constraints reported.

evidence.text: Our pension plan continues to meet all funding requirements under Employee Retirement Income Security Act regulations.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs are not a significant factor, with no notable impact on sales or supply chain reported.

evidence.text: Our pension plan continues to meet all funding requirements under Employee Retirement Income Security Act regulations.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is minimal, with no significant financial or operational impact expected.

evidence.text: Our pension plan continues to meet all funding requirements under Employee Retirement Income Security Act regulations.
